article.body_preview:
Dhaka, July 19 (UNI) The Asian Development Bank (ADB) Wednesday said the Bangladeshi economy is forecast to grow faster than initially expected in 2023, following better export performance.

ADB in its Asian Development Outlook (ADO) for July 2023 says the Bangladeshi economy will grow at least 6 percent in 2023-2024, up from the bank's April forecast of 5.3 percent.

For the current 2023-2024 fiscal, the Manila-based lender has kept the forecast for the growth of Bangladesh's economy unchanged

llm_connections.evidence_quote: reflects strong net exports as imports fell more sharply than expected and export growth slowed less than expected
llm_connections.scope_justification: The ADB statement attributes higher growth to 'strong net exports' driven by country-level changes in imports and exports, which directly matches the feature's national trade-balance scope.

verification.quote_best_match: reflects strong net exports as imports fell more sharply than expected and export growth slowed less than expected,"
verification.judge_reasoning: The article directly quotes the ADB: "...reflects strong net exports as imports fell more sharply than expected and export growth slowed less than expected," which explicitly links falling imports to stronger net exports, and this statement refers to the Bangladeshi economy (country-level).
